title: The Bacteria in Your Gums Can Reach Your Breast Tumors. Scientists Have Finally Mapped How.
description: A 2026 Johns Hopkins study traced the molecular route by which a common gum disease bacterium travels through the bloodstream, docks onto breast cancer cells via a specific protein-sugar lock-and-key mechanism, and accelerates both tumor growth and metastasis — with especially dangerous effects in women carrying BRCA1 mutations. The pathway is now mapped in extraordinary detail. Whether it's killing people at scale, or is the most elaborately described biological curiosity in recent oncology, remains fiercely contested. Here's what the evidence actually says.
heroImageAlt: Editorial illustration for The Bacteria in Your Gums Can Reach Your Breast Tumors. Scientists Have Finally Mapped How.
